latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

As at the close of business on 30 June 2025, the final Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.1569
151%
As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group is a small, privately‑held AIM‑listed company that primarily manages a portfolio of niche assets, often in the industrial or specialty chemicals space. Its core activity is the acquisition, holding, and eventual sale or monetisation of these assets rather than producing a consumer product.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a niche that can offer high‑margin returns if a particular asset is successfully monetised. Its assets are usually low‑volume, high‑value, and can be attractive to larger players looking for strategic acquisitions or to investors seeking an alternative play outside the crowded growth sectors.
company_30s.current_state: Allanfield is in a cash‑management phase. The latest filings show a focus on shareholder returns, fund distribution and portfolio restructuring rather than new product launches. The market is treating the stock as a small‑cap, high‑risk investment with a very narrow investor base.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy with shareholder‑return activities: the AGM in December confirmed a distribution plan, and the September filing details a return of capital to investors. The March and July updates focus on portfolio reviews and holdings adjustments, suggesting a steady‑state approach rather than aggressive growth.
A second theme is capital optimisation: the March net‑asset‑value report and the September fund receipt are both aimed at improving liquidity and reducing leverage.
The third thread is stability: the company has avoided any major product launches or market‑expansion announcements, reinforcing a defensive posture.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but watchful – the community is largely silent, with only a handful of older posts hinting at potential upside.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few older posts from 2016 and 2011 praised the company’s early IPO pricing and hinted at a discounted entry point for long‑term investors.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: The lack of recent catalysts and the focus on cash‑return activities make many wary that the company is simply preserving capital rather than driving growth.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the past year sentiment has stayed flat; there has been no surge in chatter even after the latest AGM announcement. The market remains largely indifferent.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat, indicating that any new announcement could generate a surprise spike.

near_term_catalysts: An upcoming quarterly report could reveal a new asset acquisition or a strategic partnership that would justify a price lift.
A potential share‑buyback or additional distribution announced at the next AGM would create a buying opportunity.
If the company announces a partnership with a larger industrial player to monetise one of its key assets, that could trigger a rally.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a breakout above the recent 20‑day moving average accompanied by a volume spike. Also monitor any press release that mentions a new asset or a change in dividend policy.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in a late‑stage hold‑down phase; it has already survived a deep decline and is now awaiting a catalyst to reverse the trend. Confirmation will likely come from a clear business announcement rather than a technical move.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company can secure a new asset or partnership, the stock could move to the mid‑point of its pre‑crash range, delivering a sizeable upside while keeping the downside limited to the current level. The contrarian signal suggests that a deep crash can create an asymmetric reward if the market over‑reacted.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a tangible catalyst, the stock may continue to trade at a low level, and any attempt to push it higher could be met with weak demand. The risk of stagnation is high, and the company’s focus on cash distribution may not provide the growth needed to lift the price.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is high if a catalyst arrives, but the probability of such an event is uncertain. The downside is limited to the current trading level, making the risk/reward skewed in favour of a cautious bullish stance.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear breakout above the 20‑day average with at least 2× normal volume. Size the position to 5–10% of available capital, set a stop just below the recent low to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the company announces a new asset acquisition or a significant partnership. A volume spike of 3× the average daily volume on a positive earnings or partnership announcement would be a trigger.
playbook.if_passing: None identified. The company has no immediate delisting or bankruptcy risk.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Allanfield is a cash‑management, niche‑asset holder that has survived a deep price crash; a new asset deal or partnership could spark a rebound, but the current lack of catalysts keeps the upside uncertain.
